page: Amazon Web Services r5.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system provides 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. Memory capacity totals 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per physical core. No local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance type. Network connectivity is delivered via a 25 Gbps baseline interface.

Benchmark results indicate a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core performance is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and average for Geekbench, the massive core count drives strong multi-core performance. Multi-core stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with both cached small-block and uncached large-block read and write operations scoring in the top 25%. This high memory throughput supports strong database operations and Redis SET performance, which also rank in the top 25%.

The instance exhibits notable tradeoffs in compute-intensive tasks. Large-model LLM text generation is poor, with 7B model generation falling into the bottom 10%, and single-threaded gzip compression is weak. However, static web serving throughput is strong. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, the absence of local storage, and strong multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale databases, in-memory caches like Redis, and distributed data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a 25 Gbps baseline network speed, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal a significant contrast between weak single-core performance and strong multi-core execution, with multi-core stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, placing in the top 25% for both read and write operations, which directly translates to strong Redis SET and database operations. Conversely, LLM text generation is weak to poor. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, large databases, and in-memory caching rather than single-threaded or generative AI workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A memory-optimized platform delivering high-capacity DDR4 RAM and strong multi-core throughput for demanding database and in-memory caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM on Intel Xeon 8259CL architecture
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive processing
